name: Detectron2 (Meta AI Object Detection and Segmentation)

auth.notes: Local library; pretrained weights downloaded from Meta CDN

performance.notes: GPU required; NVIDIA Ampere GPU for best performance; varies per model complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Meta AI's object detection and segmentation framework. from detectron2.engine import DefaultPredictor. from detectron2.config import get_cfg. from detectron2 import model_zoo. cfg = get_cfg(). cfg.merge_from_file(model_zoo.get_config_file('COCO-Detection/faster_rcnn_R_50_FPN_3x.yaml')). cfg.MODEL.WEIGHTS = model_zoo.get_checkpoint_url(...). predictor = DefaultPredictor(cfg). outputs = predictor(image). Models: Faster R-CNN, Mask R-CNN, RetinaNet, Panoptic FPN, DETR. Training: DefaultTrainer. PointRend, DensePose, DeepLab. Build-on-PyTorch C++/CUDA extensions.

gotchas: Build from source required — pip install may fail on non-standard environments
CUDA required — no CPU-only mode for practical use
Complex configuration system (YAML + CfgNode) has steep learning curve
Development has slowed — some newer architectures not included
Installation issues with CUDA/PyTorch version mismatches are common
best_when: Research requiring Meta's canonical detection implementations
Mask R-CNN, Panoptic Segmentation, DensePose tasks
When model zoo includes the exact pretrained model needed
Academic papers replicating Meta AI results
avoid_when: Production deployments (use Ultralytics YOLOv8 for simpler deployment)
summary: Detectron2 is Meta AI's object detection/segmentation framework. DefaultPredictor(cfg).predict(image). Models: Faster R-CNN, Mask R-CNN, RetinaNet, Panoptic FPN, DensePose, PointRend. Model zoo with COCO pretrained weights. Apache-2.0. CUDA required. Build from source — complex installation. Research-grade: for production use Ultralytics YOLOv8. Best for academic research and Meta AI canonical detection implementations.
